memoryArtifacts.headline: Info on Warin the Bald
memoryArtifacts.description:
NAME: To the Normans he was known as "Warin the Bald" and to the Welsh "Warin Voel". (J-Morris, Shropshire Genealogies, vol. 7 p. 3613)

OFFICE: He was made Governor of Shrewsbury by Roger de Montgo …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz於1079年出生在法國德 艾斯特莫瑟勒梅斯時，他的父親Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I29歲，母親Aimeria de Montgomery24歲。他於大約1107年與Maud Peverell在英國英格蘭史洛普爾廷頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1168年在英國英格蘭哈普爾布拉苗利享年89歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾布羅姆利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz于1079年出生在法国德 艾斯特莫瑟勒梅斯时，他的父亲Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I29岁，母亲Aimeria de Montgomery24岁。他于大约1107年与Maud Peverell在英国英格兰史洛普爾廷頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1168年在英国英格兰哈普爾布拉苗利享年89岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾布羅姆利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Metz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a short form of the female personal name Mechthild, composed of ancient Germanic maht ‘might, strength’ + hild ‘strife, battle’.

German, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Metz in Lorraine, which took its name from a Gaulish tribe, the Mediomatrici; in the name of the place this became abbreviated to Mettis, hence the modern name.
German: from Mätz, a short form of the personal name Matthias (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Mette
meanings: North German: from a pet form of the female personal name Mechthild (see Metz ).
North German: occupational name for a miller, from Middle Low German matte, mette ‘dry measure’.
French: topographic name from Old French mete ‘boundary stone, boundary’ (from Latin meta ‘bounded estate’).

variants.name: Metzer
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Metz (see   Metz  ).
Dutch: occupational name for a bricklayer, Middle Dutch metsere.

variants.name: Metts
meanings: Americanized form of German   Metz  .

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Metze
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Gerhard, Gernot.
German:
metronymic from a short form of the personal name Mechthild (see Metz 1).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Fulk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. The name was originally a short form of various compound names containing the word volk ‘people, tribe’ (compare modern English folk). It has gradually died out of general use, but is still used in certain families, in particular the Grevilles. Fulke Greville, 1st Baron Brooke, was a leading figure at the court of Elizabeth I. See also Greville .

variants.name: Fulke
meanings: Of Germanic origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. The name was originally a short form of various compound names containing the word volk ‘people, tribe’ (compare modern English folk). It has gradually died out of general use, but is still used in certain families, in particular the Grevilles. Fulke Greville, 1st Baron Brooke, was a leading figure at the court of Elizabeth I. See also Greville .

nameMeanings.name: "The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothea  , also used independently.

nameMeanings.name: Bold"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the common Irish and Scottish surname, which is of uncertain derivation. It may originally have been a local name from the island of Bute in the Firth of Clyde, known in Gaelic as Bod (genitive case Bóid). The first known bearer of the surname is Robertus de Boyd, recorded in Scotland in the early 13th century.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz was born in 1079, in Metz, Moselle, Grand Est, France, his father, Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I, was 29 and his mother, Aimeria de Montgomery, was 24. He married Maud Peverell about 1107, in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1168, in Bramley, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 89, and was buried in Bromley, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz est né en 1079, à Metz, Moselle, Grand Est, France, son père, Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Aimeria de Montgomery, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Maud Peverell environ 1107, à Whittington, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1168, à Bramley, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 89 ans, et a été enterré à Bromley, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz 1079 in Metz, Moselle, Großer Osten, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I 29 und seine Mutter Aimeria de Montgomery 24. Er hat um 1107 in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Maud Peverell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1168 im Alter von 89 Jahren in Bramley, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bromley, Shropshire, England begraben.

Lui sposato Maud Peverell circa 1107, nel Whittington, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metzが1079年にフランスグランド エストモセルメッツで生まれた時，父Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz Iは29歳，母Aimeria de Montgomeryは24歳であった。約1107年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンでMaud Peverellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1168年にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアブラムリーで89歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアブロムリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz(이)가 1079년 프랑스 그랜드 에스트 모셀 멧즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Aimeria de Montgomery(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1107년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에서 Maud Peverell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1168년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 브램리에서 89세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 브롬리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz nasceu em 1079, em Metz, Moselle, Grand Est, França, seu pai, Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Aimeria de Montgomery, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud Peverell aproximadamente 1107, em Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1168, em Bramley, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 89 anos, e foi sepultado em Bromley, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz родился в 1079 г. в местности мец, моселл, гранд эст, Франция, его отцу по имени Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Aimeria de Montgomery, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Peverell около 1107 г. в местности уиттингтон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1168 г. в местности брамли, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 89 лет и был похоронен в местности бромли, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz nació en 1079, en Metz, Moselle, Grand Est, Francia, su papá, Lord Warin "The Sheriff" de Metz I, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Aimeria de Montgomery, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Maud Peverell aproximadamente 1107, en Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1168, en Bramley, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 89 años, y fue sepultado en Bromley, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
